text: movies_anticipated() wraps endpoint /movies/anticipated. shows_anticipated() wraps endpoint /shows/anticipated.

title: Anticipated media — anticipated_media
text: limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

title: Anticipated media — anticipated_media
text: functions access automatically updated lists provided trakt.tv. function comes two flavors: Shows movies. following descriptions adapted directly API reference. Popular: Popularity calculated using rating percentage number ratings. Trending: Returns movies/shows watched right now. Movies/shows users returned first. Played: Returns played (single user can watch multiple times) movies/shows specified time period. Watched: Returns watched (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Collected: Returns collected (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Anticipated: Returns anticipated movies/shows based number lists movie/show appears . functions Played, Watched, Collected Played return additional variables besides media information: watcher_count, play_count, collected_count, collector_count.

Possible values "weekly", "monthly", "yearly", "". filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

text: query character(1): Match titles descriptions. years numeric: single 4-digit year length-2 range, e.g. 2016 c(2000, 2010). genres, languages, countries, certifications character: One genre slugs, language codes, country codes, certification slugs. subgenres character: One subgenre slugs (technical preview). runtimes numeric: Runtime minutes; single value length-2 range. studio_ids, network_ids integer: One Trakt studio / network IDs. ratings, votes numeric: Trakt rating (0-100) / vote-count (0-100000) range. tmdb_ratings, tmdb_votes numeric: TMDB rating (0.0-10.0) / vote-count range. imdb_ratings, imdb_votes numeric: IMDb rating (0.0-10.0) / vote-count range. rt_meters, rt_user_meters numeric: Rotten Tomatoes tomatometer / audience-score range (0-100). Movies . metascores numeric: Metacritic score range (0-100). Movies . networks character: One network names (e.g. "Netflix"), validated trakt_networks. precise matching prefer network_ids. status character: One show statuses: "returning series", "continuing", "production", "planned", "upcoming", "pilot", "canceled", "ended". episode_types character: One episode types: "standard", "series_premiere", "season_premiere", "mid_season_finale", "mid_season_premiere", "season_finale", "series_finale".

title: Get all comments of a thing — media_comments
text: id character(1): ID item requested. Preferably trakt ID (e.g. 1429). options trakt.tv slug (e.g. "-wire") imdb ID (e.g. "tt0306414"). Can also length greater 1, case function called id values separately result combined. See vignette("tRakt") details. sort character(1) ["newest"]: Comment sort order, one "newest", "oldest", "likes" "replies". extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). season, episode integer(1) [1L]: season episode number. longer, e.g. 1:5, function vectorized output combined. may result lot API calls. Use wisely.

title: Most played media — played_media
text: limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). period character(1) ["weekly"]: period filter . Possible values "weekly", "monthly", "yearly", "". filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

title: Most played media — played_media
text: functions access automatically updated lists provided trakt.tv. function comes two flavors: Shows movies. following descriptions adapted directly API reference. Popular: Popularity calculated using rating percentage number ratings. Trending: Returns movies/shows watched right now. Movies/shows users returned first. Played: Returns played (single user can watch multiple times) movies/shows specified time period. Watched: Returns watched (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Collected: Returns collected (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Anticipated: Returns anticipated movies/shows based number lists movie/show appears . functions Played, Watched, Collected Played return additional variables besides media information: watcher_count, play_count, collected_count, collector_count.

title: Common API parameters — trakt_api_common_parameters
text: parameters used extensively throughout package required many API methods. documented one place, intended make individual function documentation consistent.

title: Common API parameters — trakt_api_common_parameters
text: id character(1): ID item requested. Preferably trakt ID (e.g. 1429). options trakt.tv slug (e.g. "-wire") imdb ID (e.g. "tt0306414"). Can also length greater 1, case function called id values separately result combined. See vignette("tRakt") details. extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). type character(1): Either "shows" "movies". season/episode-specific functions, values seasons episodes also allowed. user character(1): Target username (slug). Defaults "", OAuth user. Can also length greater 1, case function called user values separately result combined. period character(1) ["weekly"]: period filter . Possible values "weekly", "monthly", "yearly", "". limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. season, episode integer(1) [1L]: season episode number. longer, e.g. 1:5, function vectorized output combined. may result lot API calls. Use wisely. start_date character(1): date past count updates. date supplied, default use yesterday relative current date. Value must either standard YYYY-MM-DD format object class Date, coerced via .character(). sort character(1) ["newest"]: Comment sort order, one "newest", "oldest", "likes" "replies". item_id character(1): ID item looking . query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

title: Trending media — trending_media
text: limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

title: Trending media — trending_media
text: functions access automatically updated lists provided trakt.tv. function comes two flavors: Shows movies. following descriptions adapted directly API reference. Popular: Popularity calculated using rating percentage number ratings. Trending: Returns movies/shows watched right now. Movies/shows users returned first. Played: Returns played (single user can watch multiple times) movies/shows specified time period. Watched: Returns watched (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Collected: Returns collected (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Anticipated: Returns anticipated movies/shows based number lists movie/show appears . functions Played, Watched, Collected Played return additional variables besides media information: watcher_count, play_count, collected_count, collector_count.

title: Most watched media — watched_media
text: limit integer(1) [10L]: Number items return. Must greater 0 coerced via .integer(). extended character: Level detail API response. "min" (default): Minimal info (title, year, IDs). Omits extended query param. "full": Complete info including overview, ratings, runtime, etc. "images": Minimal info plus image URLs (returned list-column). "full,images": Complete info plus images. "metadata": Collection endpoints ; adds video/audio metadata. Multiple values can combined comma-separated string (e.g. "full,images") character vector (e.g. c("full", "images")). period character(1) ["weekly"]: period filter . Possible values "weekly", "monthly", "yearly", "". filters trakt_filters object created filters_movies(), filters_shows(), filters_episodes() refines items returned. See filters full set supported filters. Supplying filters individual arguments (genres, years, networks, ...) soft-deprecated favour argument; given, filters takes precedence. query character(1): Search string titles descriptions. search_query() fields searched depending type media. See API docs full reference. years character | integer: 4-digit year (2010) range, e.g. "2010-2020". Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately, e.g. c(2010, 2020). genres character(n): Genre  slug(s). See trakt_genres table genres. Multiple values allowed concatenated. languages character(n): Two-letter language code(s). Also see trakt_languages available languages (code name). countries character(n): Two-letter country code(s). See trakt_countries. runtimes character | integer: Integer range minutes, e.g. 30-90. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. ratings character | integer:  Integer range 0 100. Can also integer vector length two coerced appropriately. Note user-supplied ratings range 1 10, yet ratings site scaled range 1 100. certifications character(n): Certification(s) like pg-13. Multiple values allowed. Use trakt_certifications reference. Note different certifications shows movies. networks character(n): (Shows ) Network name like HBO. See trakt_networks list known networks. status character(n): (Shows ) status shows. One "returning series", "production", "planned", "canceled", "ended".

title: Most watched media — watched_media
text: functions access automatically updated lists provided trakt.tv. function comes two flavors: Shows movies. following descriptions adapted directly API reference. Popular: Popularity calculated using rating percentage number ratings. Trending: Returns movies/shows watched right now. Movies/shows users returned first. Played: Returns played (single user can watch multiple times) movies/shows specified time period. Watched: Returns watched (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Collected: Returns collected (unique users) movies/shows specified time period. Anticipated: Returns anticipated movies/shows based number lists movie/show appears . functions Played, Watched, Collected Played return additional variables besides media information: watcher_count, play_count, collected_count, collector_count.

title: tRakt 0.19.0
text: lists_popular() / lists_trending() gain type argument ("personal" "official", default "personal") now send required path segment. trakt.tv API changed: bare lists/popular / lists/trending endpoints now return empty HTTP 204 response, list type must specified explicitly (lists/popular/:type). Previously functions silently returned empty tibble. Fix error networks (fixed-vocabulary) filter validation used dynamic-list functions (shows_anticipated(), movies_popular(), etc.): filter value matched several entries differing case duplicated API’s lookup data (e.g. "Netflix" / "NETFLIX") aborted “Result must length 1, 2”. values now resolve single canonical spelling. trakt_get() now retries transient server errors (HTTP 500, 502, 503, 504) connection failures, just httr2 defaults (429, 503). trakt.tv API intermittently returns gateway errors (502/504) succeed retry; previously surfaced hard failures. New trakt_api_available(): lightweight, non-erroring check whether API reachable (returns TRUE/FALSE). used guard runnable documentation examples execute API skipped (rather failing) outages offline. Documentation examples public (non-authenticated) endpoints now run conditionally via @examplesIf trakt_api_available() instead hidden \dontrun{}. exercised R CMD check example() API reachable, skipped otherwise, upstream flakiness longer produces spurious example failures. Examples genuinely require authentication remain \dontrun{}. Tests: vcr cassettes now replay-. previous re_record_interval 30 days caused CI make live API calls (fail upstream hiccup drift) cassettes aged past interval. Re-record cassettes deliberately locally instead. Vignettes (Getting Started, Show Analysis: 24) now pre-computed: authored vignettes/*.Rmd.orig (live API code) knitted static vignettes/*.Rmd via vignettes/precompile.R. shipped vignettes contain baked-output make API calls check/build time, upstream outage longer breaks R CMD check pkgdown site. Regenerate deliberately make vignettes (Rscript vignettes/precompile.R).

title: tRakt 0.18.0
text: guest_stars argument shows_people() / seasons_people() / episodes_people() now deprecated (via lifecycle::deprecate_warn()). trakt.tv API stopped returning separate guest_stars array — guest cast now included cast. argument -op pending removal future release. Fix vectorisation bug movies_related() / shows_related(): called multiple IDs, internal recursion forwarded extended argument positionally limit, producing malformed ?limit=min URLs empty responses non-first IDs (subsequently erroring column-flattening). seasons_ratings() adapts new multi-source rating response shape. API now returns separate sub-objects per source (trakt, tmdb, imdb, metascore, rotten_tomatoes). trakt source promoted top level (preserving existing rating / votes / distribution columns), external scores surfaced <source>_<field> columns (tmdb_rating, imdb_link, metascore_rating, rotten_tomatoes_state, etc.). Missing values returned NA. unpack_user() now renames user’s trakt id user_trakt avoid colliding media list trakt id unpacked user bind_cols()’d alongside identifiers. Previously produced auto-repaired column names trakt...17. Affects functions embed user data: *_lists(), user_lists(), user_list(), user_comments(), user_summary(), comments, media_watching(), list-popular endpoints. user_list() longer errors single-list responses embedded user profile contains NULL fields (e.g. age, vip_cover_image); now coerced NA tibble conversion. shows_people() / seasons_people() / episodes_people() longer return separate guest_stars table called guest_stars = TRUE, trakt API stopped returning array. Guest cast now mixed cast. guest_stars argument currently -op pending deprecation decision. Show results now include plex_guid plex_slug ID columns, subgenres list column, new fields tagline original_title. Nested images colors objects dropped show data don’t fit tabular output. search_query() search_id() now return properly structured show data extended = "min" extended = "full". Token refresh failures (e.g. expired revoked refresh tokens) now fall back device authentication instead erroring. seasons_season() renamed seasons_episodes(), wrapping endpoint shows/:id/seasons/:season?translations=. seasons_season() now wraps new(?) endpoint shows/:id/seasons/:season/info. Start using {vcr} recording API responses. Bump testthat dependency >=3.0.0, necessary since adopting 3e. Swap purrr-style anonymous functions ~ .x base-R \(x) x-style ones Substitute map_df() map() + list_rbind() "min" longer sends ?extended=min API (never valid API value). Instead, query parameter omitted entirely, matching API’s documented behavior. New values "images", "metadata", combinations like c("full", "images") "full,images" now supported. extended includes "images", image data preserved list-column instead dropped. functions now use validate_extended() internally instead match.arg(), providing clearer error messages invalid values.
